sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 135, 136, 137, 138 & 139 OF 2020 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ For orders as to non-prosecution as none is making compliance on office objections since 10.11.2020 (Flagged “A”) 23.01.2026 Dr. Imtiaz Ali advocate holds brief for Dr. Shahnawaz Memon, advocate for the applicant These references are pending since 2020 and remains listed for orders as to non-prosecution ever since. On 15.01.2021 one week’s time was granted to do the needful, however, today almost six years later, situation remains the same and matters are listed for orders as to non- prosecution. It appears that the applicant department is not interested in proceeding with these matters. Accordingly, in view of judgment of the Supreme Court in the case of C.I.R. vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, these reference are dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Office is instructed to place copy of this order in connected matters. Judge Judge Amjad
